answers.answer: The task is already complete. rate_limit_headers() at server.py:682-707 builds all four X-RateLimit-* headers. handle_api() at server.py:4597-4612 applies them to both success responses (status < 400, line 4604-4605) and 429 rate_limit_exceeded responses (lines 4608-4611). All five rate-limited POST routes flow through handle_api() and _enforce_write_controls(), so they all receive headers. There are no gaps in the implementation.
answers.uncertainty: The task title says 'Add rate limit headers' which could imply they don't exist yet, but the code clearly shows they are already implemented.

answers.answer: Close and reopen endpoints do NOT exist in this codebase. resolve_write_route_category() at server.py:551-571 maps exactly 5 POST routes: /v1/markets (create), /v1/markets/{id}/resolve, /v1/markets/{id}/comments, /v1/markets/{id}/orders/probability-edit, and /v1/markets/{id}/orders/event-trade. route_request() at server.py:4316-4495 confirms there are no close or reopen routes. The breadth test at line 6888 already covers all 5 existing rate-limited routes. The question's premise about 'close and reopen' routes is incorrect — they don't exist.
answers.uncertainty: None — exhaustive search of route_request and resolve_write_route_category confirms no close/reopen endpoints.

answers.answer: The X-RateLimit-Policy header is part of the established contract. rate_limit_headers() always includes it (server.py:706), and existing tests assert both its presence and absence (test_bayes_market_api.py:6801, 6811). The task description mentions three headers (Limit, Remaining, Reset) but the implementation provides all four including Policy. Validating all four is appropriate since they are part of the existing contract and removing Policy would be a regression.
answers.uncertainty: The task owner might have intended only three headers, but since Policy is already implemented and tested, including it is the safe choice.

answers.answer: This is the correct and intended behavior. rate_limit_headers() at server.py:684 explicitly returns an empty dict when RATE_LIMIT_PER_MIN <= 0 or agent_id is empty. This is validated by dedicated tests: test_probability_edit_http_omits_rate_limit_headers_when_limiter_disabled (line 6959) and test_probability_edit_http_omits_rate_limit_headers_without_agent_id (line 6970). The design is deliberate — headers signal rate-limit state only when rate limiting is actually active and an agent is identified.
answers.uncertainty: None — the behavior is explicitly tested and matches the design intent of conditional header emission.

assumption_checks.impact: The rate limit header feature is fully implemented. rate_limit_headers() builds headers, handle_api() applies them to success and 429 responses on all rate-limited routes.
All 5 rate-limited POST routes (not 7 — close and reopen don't exist) receive headers through handle_api(). The breadth test covers all 5.

Close and reopen routes don't exist, so there are no missing test cases to add. The breadth test already covers all 5 rate-limited routes. The task may already be fully complete with no changes needed.
Only POST routes are rate-limited. resolve_write_route_category() returns None for non-POST methods at line 553-554.
